{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Payroll Officer",
  "description": "Sterling Oil Exploration &amp; Energy Production Company Limited (SEEPCO)&nbsp;was established in 2003 and headquartered in Lagos, Nigeria, is one of the fastest-growing oil exploration and production companies in Africa. With operations in multiple countries and expanding.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Payroll Officer
Job Location:&nbsp;VI, Lagos

Job Description


	Accurately process monthly payroll within approved timelines.
	Maintain and update employee payroll records and salary information.
	Calculate salaries, overtime, bonuses, allowances, deductions, and final settlements.
	Reconcile payroll reports and resolve any discrepancies.
	Prepare payroll reports for management review and audit purposes.
	Safeguard the confidentiality of payroll information and employee records.
	Monitor attendance, leave records, and overtime data for payroll processing.
	Address employee inquiries related to payroll and resolve issues promptly.
	Support payroll audits and ensure compliance with labor laws and company policies.
	Maintain proper documentation and filing of payroll records.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Human Resources, Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or related field.
	Proven experience as a Payroll Officer or similar role.
	Strong knowledge of payroll processes and statutory regulations.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and payroll/HRIS systems.
	Strong numerical and analytical skills.
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
	Good communication and confidentiality management skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to:&nbsp;careers@mgt-energy.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Payroll Officer Job at Sterling Oil Exploration & Energy Production Company Limited




        
    

        
        Sterling Oil Exploration & Energy Production Company Limited (SEEPCO) was established in 2003 and headquartered in Lagos, Nigeria, is one of the fastest-growing oil exploration and production companies in Africa. With operations in multiple countries and expanding.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Payroll Officer
Job Location: VI, Lagos

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Accurately process monthly payroll within approved timelines.
	Maintain and update employee payroll records and salary information.
	Calculate salaries, overtime, bonuses, allowances, deductions, and final settlements.
	Reconcile payroll reports and resolve any discrepancies.
	Prepare payroll reports for management review and audit purposes.
	Safeguard the confidentiality of payroll information and employee records.
	Monitor attendance, leave records, and overtime data for payroll processing.
	Address employee inquiries related to payroll and resolve issues promptly.
	Support payroll audits and ensure compliance with labor laws and company policies.
	Maintain proper documentation and filing of payroll records.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or related field.
	Proven experience as a Payroll Officer or similar role.
	Strong knowledge of payroll processes and statutory regulations.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and payroll/HRIS systems.
	Strong numerical and analytical skills.
	High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
	Good communication and confidentiality management skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Civil Engineer",
  "description": "Eko Maintenance Limited is a rapidly growing Facility Management Company that specializes in the maintenance of high-rise building facilities with an office located in Victoria Island, Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Civil Engineer
Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The role involves overseeing construction and finishing operations, with a strong focus on high-rise building maintenance and fa&ccedil;ade works.


Responsibilities


	Prepare detailed drawings showing both the appearance and interior structure of a building project. eg. Plumbing, Ventilating System, Electrical System, etc.
	Control project from start to finish to ensure high quality, innovative and functional design.
	Lead, supervise, and effectively coordinate a technical team specializing in construction and finishing works.
	Ensure that the structure is safe, stable and meet engineering standards.
	Supervise installation of aluminum Fa&ccedil;ade,Alucobond and glass work, finishing of doors and windows partition.
	Develop initial estimates on structure costs, building time and special requirements.


Relevant work experience/Qualification


	Candidates should possess HND / B.Sc Degree in Civil Engineering (Master&rsquo;s Degree in Civil Engineering or an associated field is an additional advantage).
	4 &ndash; 6 years of relevant work experienceon finishing buildings and construction.
	Knowledge and proficiency in industry-specific software, like AutoCAD Civil 3D, and other computer-aided design software..
	Good communication and team collaboration skills.


Salary &amp; Benefits


	Salary: N250,000 - N300,000 per month.&nbsp;
	HMO Coverage
	Pension
	Annual Leave/Allowance.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment3@ekomaintenance.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Civil Engineer Job at Eko Maintenance Limited




        
    

        
        Eko Maintenance Limited is a rapidly growing Facility Management Company that specializes in the maintenance of high-rise building facilities with an office located in Victoria Island, Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Civil Engineer
Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The role involves overseeing construction and finishing operations, with a strong focus on high-rise building maintenance and façade works.


Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Prepare detailed drawings showing both the appearance and interior structure of a building project. eg. Plumbing, Ventilating System, Electrical System, etc.
	Control project from start to finish to ensure high quality, innovative and functional design.
	Lead, supervise, and effectively coordinate a technical team specializing in construction and finishing works.
	Ensure that the structure is safe, stable and meet engineering standards.
	Supervise installation of aluminum Façade,Alucobond and glass work, finishing of doors and windows partition.
	Develop initial estimates on structure costs, building time and special requirements.


Relevant work experience/Qualification


	Candidates should possess HND / B.Sc Degree in Civil Engineering (Master’s Degree in Civil Engineering or an associated field is an additional advantage).
	4 – 6 years of relevant work experienceon finishing buildings and construction.
	Knowledge and proficiency in industry-specific software, like AutoCAD Civil 3D, and other computer-aided design software..
	Good communication and team collaboration skills.


Salary & Benefits


	Salary: N250,000 - N300,000 per month. 
	HMO Coverage
	Pension
	Annual Leave/Allowance.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Air Operations Supervisor",
  "description": "Shell Petroleum Development Company (SPDC) is the pioneer and leader of the petroleum industry in Nigeria. We invest heavily in our employees, which is reflected in our industry-leading development programme and our commitment to see our employees&rsquo; ideas travel and come to fruition.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Air Operations Supervisor

Job Requisition ID:&nbsp;R202222
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Job Type: Full-time
Job Family Group:&nbsp;Ungraded NTBE
Worker Type:&nbsp;Regular
Business Unit:&nbsp;HR Corporate Functions
Experience Level:&nbsp;Experienced Professionals

Job Description


	This is a unique opportunity to bring your aviation expertise and management skills to help Shell drive its operational excellence. Join a team of subject matter experts, applying your aviation expertise, partnering with business delivery teams to influence stakeholder decisions to optimize resources, preserve and create value for Shell, and ensure air transport operations are delivered at a risk level that is as low as reasonably practicable (ALARP).
	As an Aviation Operations Supervisor, this position provides Air Transport Technical Authority support and manages air transport exposure and services for Shell business units operating in Nigeria.
	Your role is accountable for the management, oversight, and continuous improvement of contracted air operations; for ensuring robust air operations emergency response; and for providing technical expertise and assurance. In addition, you will also be responsible for delivering air transport safety and cost?performance targets while functioning as the Air Operations Supervisor for all the air transport activities in Nigeria.


What You&rsquo;ll Be Doing


	Your role is accountable for delivering air transport technical authority support, contract management, aviation assurance, emergency response, and safety oversight, along with additional responsibilities as assigned by the Head of Aircraft Services &ndash; Central. Key responsibilities include, but are not limited to, the following:


Air Transport Technical Authority / Asssurance:


	Achieve and maintain Air Transport technical competency and be prepared to provide technical support, augment, assist, or on a short-term basis fill-in for other individual technical authorities as directed by the Head of Aircraft Services &ndash; Central
	Provide direction, advice, and support to business unit staff to optimize the efficiency of Air Transport operations and drive the sharing and implementation of global best practices and the Shell standards
	Provide guidance, direction, and mentoring to Company contracted air operators that provide air transport capabilities and execute air transport operations on the Company&rsquo;s behalf while ensuring compliance with Company and Industry standards and the assurance of those operations
	Establish and maintain regular engagements with business unit leaders to ensure awareness and understanding of air transport safety, operational issues, and the management of air transport risk
	Manage the delivery of the Safety Case, its associated bowties (or equivalent), and integrate it with the annual air transport safety plan locally by providing the framework to ensure a robust safety culture exists within all air transport operations that are supported
	Provide assurance that air transport operations are being carried out in compliance with local and Shell aviation standards


Contract Management:


	Manage both strategic and tactical contracts as the Contract Holder where appropriate and interface with air operators / vendors to ensure contractual compliance
	Provide leadership, guidance, and ensure air operator compliance with Shell standards and conduct regular air operator engagements and performance assessments
	Direct and facilitate the development of metrics, scorecards, and performance reporting of air transport related Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


Health, Safety, Security &amp; Environment (HSSE) / Emergence Response:


	Apply and implement the Air Transport Safety Case, ensuring that it is adhered to for all major contracts and activities
	Participate in safety assessments, incident reporting and investigations, and incident risk assessments
	Conduct facility (airport, airfield, heliport, helideck) audits and inspections in accordance with the Shell standards
	Support the Head of Aircraft Services &ndash; Central with specialist advice and technical expertise to facilitate the development, adoption, and effective implementation of Aviation Safety Management Systems (SMS) and tools appropriate to the air transport activities undertaken across the Central Region, meeting the requirements of the Shell standards
	Provide a rapid and sustained aviation response capability to support critical Emergency Response requirements in support of assigned business units
	Lead and direct the delivery of air transport resources during Emergency Response activities.&nbsp;
	Plan and coordinate SAR/MEDEVAC capabilities that are adequate and available to meet Shell air transport response requirements and needs in the execution of SAR/MEDEVAC missions


What We Need From You


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree is desired, plus a valid ATPL (preferred), CPL, or A&amp;P engineer license; military equivalents accepted
	Extensive years of aviation experience, including leadership roles in the oil industry, aircraft operations, or equivalent military organizations, with a strong record of leading diverse, inclusive teams for compliance with local aviation regulations and recognized industry standards
	Possess aviation maintenance experience, with awareness of scheduled/unscheduled maintenance, maintenance quality assurance principles, and serviceability and dispatch requirements
	Bring supply chain/logistics experience, including awareness of time-distance considerations, aviation logistical support, budgeting, and hazardous material by air handling
	Demonstrate project management experience, with strong understanding of project management techniques, budgeting, and scheduling
	Exhibit strong public speaking/briefing skills and the ability to communicate effectively with senior management, external bodies, and employees at all levels
	Be physically able to work offshore, maintain Helicopter Underwater Escape Training certification, and conduct offshore personnel transfers via personnel hoisting devices and other systems
	(Desired) Offshore helicopter air transport experience
	(Desired) Graduate of civilian or military courses in accident investigation, aviation safety/SMS, maintenance management or QA, experimental test pilot, military maintenance test pilot, factory pilot or maintenance, and AS9100:2016 Lead Auditor (REV D)
	Fluent in English.


&nbsp;

What we offer&nbsp;


	You bring your skills and experience to Shell and in return you work with talented, committed people on one of the most important challenges facing our planet.
	You&rsquo;ll have the opportunity to develop the skills you need to grow in an environment where we value honesty, integrity, and respect for one another.
	You&rsquo;ll be able to balance your priorities as you become the best version of yourself.&nbsp;
	Progress as a person as we work on the energy transition together
	Continuously grow the transferable skills you need to get ahead
	Work at the forefront of technology, trends, and practices
	Collaborate with experienced colleagues with unique expertise
	Achieve your balance in a value-led culture that encourages you to be the best version of yourself
	Benefit from flexible working hours, and the possibility of remote/mobile working
	Perform at your best with a competitive starting salary and annual performance related salary increase &ndash; our pay and benefits packages are considered to be among the best in the world
	Take advantage of paid parental leave, including for non-birthing parents
	Grow as you progress through diverse career opportunities in national and international teams
	Gain access to a wide range of training and development programs.

Air Operations Supervisor Job at Shell Petroleum Development Company (SPDC)




        
    

        
        Shell Petroleum Development Company (SPDC) is the pioneer and leader of the petroleum industry in Nigeria. We invest heavily in our employees, which is reflected in our industry-leading development programme and our commitment to see our employees’ ideas travel and come to fruition.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Air Operations Supervisor

Job Requisition ID: R202222
Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Full-time
Job Family Group: Ungraded NTBE
Worker Type: Regular
Business Unit: HR Corporate Functions
Experience Level: Experienced Professionals

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	This is a unique opportunity to bring your aviation expertise and management skills to help Shell drive its operational excellence. Join a team of subject matter experts, applying your aviation expertise, partnering with business delivery teams to influence stakeholder decisions to optimize resources, preserve and create value for Shell, and ensure air transport operations are delivered at a risk level that is as low as reasonably practicable (ALARP).
	As an Aviation Operations Supervisor, this position provides Air Transport Technical Authority support and manages air transport exposure and services for Shell business units operating in Nigeria.
	Your role is accountable for the management, oversight, and continuous improvement of contracted air operations; for ensuring robust air operations emergency response; and for providing technical expertise and assurance. In addition, you will also be responsible for delivering air transport safety and cost?performance targets while functioning as the Air Operations Supervisor for all the air transport activities in Nigeria.


What You’ll Be Doing


	Your role is accountable for delivering air transport technical authority support, contract management, aviation assurance, emergency response, and safety oversight, along with additional responsibilities as assigned by the Head of Aircraft Services – Central. Key responsibilities include, but are not limited to, the following:


Air Transport Technical Authority / Asssurance:


	Achieve and maintain Air Transport technical competency and be prepared to provide technical support, augment, assist, or on a short-term basis fill-in for other individual technical authorities as directed by the Head of Aircraft Services – Central
	Provide direction, advice, and support to business unit staff to optimize the efficiency of Air Transport operations and drive the sharing and implementation of global best practices and the Shell standards
	Provide guidance, direction, and mentoring to Company contracted air operators that provide air transport capabilities and execute air transport operations on the Company’s behalf while ensuring compliance with Company and Industry standards and the assurance of those operations
	Establish and maintain regular engagements with business unit leaders to ensure awareness and understanding of air transport safety, operational issues, and the management of air transport risk
	Manage the delivery of the Safety Case, its associated bowties (or equivalent), and integrate it with the annual air transport safety plan locally by providing the framework to ensure a robust safety culture exists within all air transport operations that are supported
	Provide assurance that air transport operations are being carried out in compliance with local and Shell aviation standards


Contract Management:


	Manage both strategic and tactical contracts as the Contract Holder where appropriate and interface with air operators / vendors to ensure contractual compliance
	Provide leadership, guidance, and ensure air operator compliance with Shell standards and conduct regular air operator engagements and performance assessments
	Direct and facilitate the development of metrics, scorecards, and performance reporting of air transport related Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


Health, Safety, Security & Environment (HSSE) / Emergence Response:


	Apply and implement the Air Transport Safety Case, ensuring that it is adhered to for all major contracts and activities
	Participate in safety assessments, incident reporting and investigations, and incident risk assessments
	Conduct facility (airport, airfield, heliport, helideck) audits and inspections in accordance with the Shell standards
	Support the Head of Aircraft Services – Central with specialist advice and technical expertise to facilitate the development, adoption, and effective implementation of Aviation Safety Management Systems (SMS) and tools appropriate to the air transport activities undertaken across the Central Region, meeting the requirements of the Shell standards
	Provide a rapid and sustained aviation response capability to support critical Emergency Response requirements in support of assigned business units
	Lead and direct the delivery of air transport resources during Emergency Response activities. 
	Plan and coordinate SAR/MEDEVAC capabilities that are adequate and available to meet Shell air transport response requirements and needs in the execution of SAR/MEDEVAC missions


What We Need From You


	Bachelor’s degree is desired, plus a valid ATPL (preferred), CPL, or A&P engineer license; military equivalents accepted
	Extensive years of aviation experience, including leadership roles in the oil industry, aircraft operations, or equivalent military organizations, with a strong record of leading diverse, inclusive teams for compliance with local aviation regulations and recognized industry standards
	Possess aviation maintenance experience, with awareness of scheduled/unscheduled maintenance, maintenance quality assurance principles, and serviceability and dispatch requirements
	Bring supply chain/logistics experience, including awareness of time-distance considerations, aviation logistical support, budgeting, and hazardous material by air handling
	Demonstrate project management experience, with strong understanding of project management techniques, budgeting, and scheduling
	Exhibit strong public speaking/briefing skills and the ability to communicate effectively with senior management, external bodies, and employees at all levels
	Be physically able to work offshore, maintain Helicopter Underwater Escape Training certification, and conduct offshore personnel transfers via personnel hoisting devices and other systems
	(Desired) Offshore helicopter air transport experience
	(Desired) Graduate of civilian or military courses in accident investigation, aviation safety/SMS, maintenance management or QA, experimental test pilot, military maintenance test pilot, factory pilot or maintenance, and AS9100:2016 Lead Auditor (REV D)
	Fluent in English.


 

What we offer 


	You bring your skills and experience to Shell and in return you work with talented, committed people on one of the most important challenges facing our planet.
	You’ll have the opportunity to develop the skills you need to grow in an environment where we value honesty, integrity, and respect for one another.
	You’ll be able to balance your priorities as you become the best version of yourself. 
	Progress as a person as we work on the energy transition together
	Continuously grow the transferable skills you need to get ahead
	Work at the forefront of technology, trends, and practices
	Collaborate with experienced colleagues with unique expertise
	Achieve your balance in a value-led culture that encourages you to be the best version of yourself
	Benefit from flexible working hours, and the possibility of remote/mobile working
	Perform at your best with a competitive starting salary and annual performance related salary increase – our pay and benefits packages are considered to be among the best in the world
	Take advantage of paid parental leave, including for non-birthing parents
	Grow as you progress through diverse career opportunities in national and international teams
	Gain access to a wide range of training and development programs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Senior HR Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ogba, Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Job Description


	Zenith Precision Limited is seeking a proactive and experienced Senior HR Officer to oversee and drive our HR operations.
	This role is ideal for a results-oriented professional looking to grow into a leadership position while making a measurable impact in a fast-growing organization.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage end-to-end recruitment, onboarding, and offboarding processes
	Maintain and oversee accurate employee records and HR systems
	Drive performance management and employee development initiatives
	Coordinate payroll processing and benefits administration
	Ensure compliance with Nigerian labor laws and company policies
	Handle employee relations, conflict resolution, and disciplinary processes
	Support the development and implementation of HR policies and procedure
	Lead HR initiatives that improve efficiency, engagement, and overall business performance.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Minimum of 5 years of experience in Human Resources or a related role
	Strong knowledge of HR practices, Nigerian labor laws, and payroll systems
	Proven ability to manage HR operations effectively
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and organizational skills
	Proficiency in HR software and Microsoft Office Suite
	Strong problem-solving and decision-making ability.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to:&nbsp;careers@zenithprecision.com.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Senior HR Officer Job at Zenith Precision Limited




        
    

        
        Zenith Precision Limited (RC713781) is a technical service provider since 2007; we provide complete solution in coding and marking, project management consultancy and engineering procurement services for plastic, bottling, pharmaceutical and processing plants. The company has a team of seasoned professionals with years of working experience and we have successfully delivered various projects in various manufacturing industries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior HR Officer

Job Location: Ogba, Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Zenith Precision Limited is seeking a proactive and experienced Senior HR Officer to oversee and drive our HR operations.
	This role is ideal for a results-oriented professional looking to grow into a leadership position while making a measurable impact in a fast-growing organization.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage end-to-end recruitment, onboarding, and offboarding processes
	Maintain and oversee accurate employee records and HR systems
	Drive performance management and employee development initiatives
	Coordinate payroll processing and benefits administration
	Ensure compliance with Nigerian labor laws and company policies
	Handle employee relations, conflict resolution, and disciplinary processes
	Support the development and implementation of HR policies and procedure
	Lead HR initiatives that improve efficiency, engagement, and overall business performance.


Qualifications & Experience


	Minimum of 5 years of experience in Human Resources or a related role
	Strong knowledge of HR practices, Nigerian labor laws, and payroll systems
	Proven ability to manage HR operations effectively
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and organizational skills
	Proficiency in HR software and Microsoft Office Suite
	Strong problem-solving and decision-making ability.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Field Sales Representative Job at Izili Nigeria




        
    

        
        Izili Nigeria (formerly known as Baobab+ Nigeria) is a social enterprise that provides affordable solar energy and digital solutions to underserved communities across Nigeria.


We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Field Sales Representative

Job Location: Abuja
Job type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    





Qualifications


	Interested applicant(s) should possess relevant qualification and experience.




Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
    

    

        Click here to Apply
